body:
## Documentation Gap **Package:** `Azure.ResourceManager.DevOpsInfrastructure` **Service directory:** `sdk/devopsinfrastructure/Azure.ResourceManager.DevOpsInfrastructure/` **Triggered by:** commit 601109e01 (PR #58580) by @azure-sdk ## What Changed The package version was incremented to `1.1.0-beta.1` following the first stable `1.0.0` release of `Azure.ResourceManager.DevOpsInfrastructure` (Managed DevOps Pools management library). The CHANGELOG now records the `1.0.0` stable release, but the README still lacks concrete code examples and meaningful Key concepts content suitable for a GA library. ## Gaps Found - **Examples section is empty of actual code** — the README only links to `https://aka.ms/azuresdk-net-mgmt-samples` with no inline examples showing how to create, list, get, or delete a pool - **Sample test files are not wired to the README** — `tests/Samples/Sample_DevOpsPoolCollection.cs`, `tests/Samples/Sample_DevOpsPoolResource.cs`, `tests/Samples/Sample_ResourceGroupResourceExtensions.cs`, and `tests/Samples/Sample_SubscriptionResourceExtensions.cs` contain full working code examples but use no `#region Snippet:` tags, so `Update-Snippets.ps1` cannot inject them into the README - **Key concepts section is generic** — it only links to generic Azure SDK docs and does not explain service-specific concepts such as `DevOpsPoolResource`, `DevOpsVmssFabricProfile`, `DevOpsAzureOrganizationProfile`, or `DevOpsStatelessAgentProfile` - **Authenticate the client section has no code snippet** — it only links to a quickstart guide rather than showing a short `ArmClient` + `DefaultAzureCredential` instantiation example
📐 Implementation Guide This section contains step-by-step instructions for a coding agent to implement the changes described above. ### Step 1: Modify files #### `sdk/devopsinfrastructure/Azure.ResourceManager.DevOpsInfrastructure/README.md` — Edit 1. **Authenticate the client** — replace the single-sentence link with a short snippet placeholder after the existing text: ```markdown To create an authenticated client and start interacting with Microsoft Azure resources, please install the [`Azure.Identity`](https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/blob/main/sdk/identity/Azure.Identity/README.md) package: ```dotnetcli dotnet add package Azure.Identity ``` Then instantiate an `ArmClient`: ```C# Snippet:CreateDevOpsInfrastructureClient TokenCredential cred = new DefaultAzureCredential(); ArmClient client = new ArmClient(cred); ``` ``` 2. **Key concepts** — replace the single generic link with a section that describes the main types: ```markdown ## Key concepts ### DevOpsPoolResource Represents a Managed DevOps Pool — a pool of Azure virtual machines used as Azure DevOps agents. Each pool is tied to a resource group and an Azure DevOps organization. ### DevOpsVmssFabricProfile Describes the underlying Azure VMSS-based infrastructure for the pool, including VM SKU, OS images, network settings, and OS secrets management. ### DevOpsAzureOrganizationProfile Associates the pool with one or more Azure DevOps organizations and projects. ### DevOpsStatelessAgentProfile / DevOpsStatefulAgentProfile Configures whether agents are stateless (fresh VM per job) or stateful (VM reused across jobs). Key concepts of the Microsoft Azure SDK for .NET can be found [here](https://azure.github.io/azure-sdk/dotnet_introduction.html). ``` 3. **Examples** — replace the generic link with snippet placeholders: ```markdown ## Examples ### Create a Managed DevOps Pool ```C# Snippet:CreateDevOpsPool ``` ### Get a Managed DevOps Pool ```C# Snippet:GetDevOpsPool ``` ### List all Managed DevOps Pools in a resource group ```C# Snippet:ListDevOpsPools ``` ### Delete a Managed DevOps Pool ```C# Snippet:DeleteDevOpsPool ``` ``` ### Step 2: Add or update code snippets Add `#region Snippet:` / `#endregion` tags to the existing sample files so `Update-Snippets.ps1` can inject code into the README placeholders. #### File: `sdk/devopsinfrastructure/Azure.ResourceManager.DevOpsInfrastructure/tests/Samples/Sample_DevOpsPoolCollection.cs` In the `CreateOrUpdate_PoolsCreateOrUpdate` method body, wrap the meaningful portion (after the client/resource group boilerplate) with: ```csharp #region Snippet:CreateDevOpsPool // ... (pool creation code) ... #endregion ``` Add a new minimal `Get_PoolsGet` method annotated with `[Test]` and `[Ignore("Only validating compilation of examples")]`: ```csharp [Test] [Ignore("Only validating compilation of examples")] public async Task Get_PoolsGet() { // get your azure access token TokenCredential cred = new DefaultAzureCredential(); ArmClient client = new ArmClient(cred); string subscriptionId = "a2e95d27-c161-4b61-bda4-11512c14c2c2"; string resourceGroupName = "rg"; ResourceIdentifier resourceGroupResourceId = ResourceGroupResource.CreateResourceIdentifier(subscriptionId, resourceGroupName); ResourceGroupResource resourceGroupResource = client.GetResourceGroupResource(resourceGroupResourceId); DevOpsPoolCollection collection = resourceGroupResource.GetDevOpsPools(); #region Snippet:GetDevOpsPool string poolName = "pool"; DevOpsPoolResource result = await collection.GetAsync(poolName); Console.WriteLine($"Pool name: {result.Data.Name}"); #endregion } ``` Add a new `List_PoolsList` method: ```csharp [Test] [Ignore("Only validating compilation of examples")] public async Task List_PoolsList() { TokenCredential cred = new DefaultAzureCredential(); ArmClient client = new ArmClient(cred); string subscriptionId = "a2e95d27-c161-4b61-bda4-11512c14c2c2"; string resourceGroupName = "rg"; ResourceIdentifier resourceGroupResourceId = ResourceGroupResource.CreateResourceIdentifier(subscriptionId, resourceGroupName); ResourceGroupResource resourceGroupResource = client.GetResourceGroupResource(resourceGroupResourceId); DevOpsPoolCollection collection = resourceGroupResource.GetDevOpsPools(); #region Snippet:ListDevOpsPools await foreach (DevOpsPoolResource pool in collection.GetAllAsync()) { Console.WriteLine($"Pool: {pool.Data.Name}"); } #endregion } ``` #### File: `sdk/devopsinfrastructure/Azure.ResourceManager.DevOpsInfrastructure/tests/Samples/Sample_DevOpsPoolResource.cs` Find the delete method and wrap the relevant lines: ```csharp #region Snippet:DeleteDevOpsPool ArmOperation lro = await pool.DeleteAsync(WaitUntil.Completed); #endregion ``` #### File: `sdk/devopsinfrastructure/Azure.ResourceManager.DevOpsInfrastructure/tests/Samples/Sample_ResourceGroupResourceExtensions.cs` Add a `#region Snippet:CreateDevOpsInfrastructureClient` / `#endregion` block around the `ArmClient` instantiation lines. ### Step 3: Verify README structure The README at `sdk/devopsinfrastructure/Azure.ResourceManager.DevOpsInfrastructure/README.md` already contains all required sections, but the following need content added or improved: | Section | Status | Action needed | |---|---|---| | Getting started → Install the package | ✅ Present | No change | | Getting started → Prerequisites | ✅ Present | No change | | Getting started → Authenticate the client | ⚠️ Link only | Add code snippet placeholder | | Key concepts | ⚠️ Generic link only | Add service-specific type descriptions | | Examples | ❌ No code | Add snippet placeholders for create/get/list/delete | | Troubleshooting | ✅ Present | No change | | Next steps | ✅ Present | No change | | Contributing | ✅ Present | No change | Use `sdk/keyvault/Azure.Security.KeyVault.Secrets/README.md` as the canonical reference for section formatting and tone. ### Step 4: Validate Run these commands in order. Each must succeed before proceeding to the next. 1. `dotnet build sdk/devopsinfrastructure/Azure.ResourceManager.DevOpsInfrastructure/` 2. `dotnet test sdk/devopsinfrastructure/Azure.ResourceManager.DevOpsInfrastructure/ --filter TestCategory!=Live` 3. `eng/scripts/Update-Snippets.ps1 devopsinfrastructure` — injects `#region Snippet:` code from test files into README.md placeholders. Verify the git diff shows README updated with code. 4. `eng/scripts/Export-API.ps1 devopsinfrastructure` — only needed if public types or members changed (not required for docs-only changes) 5. `dotnet format sdk/devopsinfrastructure/Azure.ResourceManager.DevOpsInfrastructure/` — ensures consistent code formatting
## Next Steps > [!TIP] > **Ready for automated implementation?** Assign this issue to **@copilot** to have Copilot coding agent implement the changes described in the Implementation Guide above
title: [DevOps Infrastructure] Docs: Add code examples and enrich README for stable 1.0.0 release
